reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.308(a)(4)(ii)(A) requires isolating healthcare clearinghouse functions, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring network isolation, strict RBAC policies, and pod security standards. NetworkPolicies can be used to restrict traffic to and from the healthcare clearinghouse components. RBAC can enforce least privilege access to these components. Pod security standards ensure that the pods running these functions adhere to security best practices. Additionally, dedicating a specific namespace for these functions can further isolate them within the cluster.
title: 164_308_a_4_ii_a: 164.308(a)(4)(ii)(A) Isolating health care clearinghouse functions
